description: Expected findings for CTLA4-Ig Fc-fusion proteins (abatacept). Blocks CD28-B7 co-stimulation, attenuating T-cell activation. For TNFR-Fc (etanercept), use anti_tnf_mab profile instead — pharmacologically equivalent to anti-TNF mAbs. Inherits Fc-mediated base findings from general_mab.

expected_findings.description: Germinal center suppression (CD28-B7 blockade impairs B-cell maturation)
expected_findings.severity_threshold: Loss of humoral immune competence = adverse
expected_findings.rationale: CD28-B7 blockade impairs B-cell maturation and germinal center responses. (Abatacept prescribing information)

expected_findings.severity_threshold: Hypogammaglobulinemia (<40% LLN) with infection susceptibility = adverse
expected_findings.rationale: Secondary to germinal center suppression from CD28-B7 blockade.

expected_findings.description: T-cell response attenuation (reduced effector T-cell function, Treg enrichment)
expected_findings.rationale: CTLA4-Ig attenuates CD28-B7 co-stimulation; T-cell functionality reduced as primary pharmacological effect. Reduced T-cell proliferation upon stimulation; Treg enrichment.
Consequence of combined T-cell attenuation and reduced humoral immunity. Risk proportional to depth and duration of immunosuppression.
Fc region of the fusion protein can activate complement. Typically mild and transient.
Common for all SC-administered protein therapeutics. Covered by general_mab base but listed for completeness.
